title: rinorea subauriculata
intro: Rinorea subauriculata, also known as the "Squirrel-bark Vine," is a type of flowering plant native to Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Rinorea subauriculata can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential interactions with other medications.
